faqs.question: How do I know if my home needs repiping?
faqs.answer: Signs that your home may need repiping include discolored water, frequent leaks, low water pressure, visible pipe corrosion, and your home being over 50 years old with original plumbing. We offer free assessments.

faqs.question: What materials do you use for repiping?
faqs.answer: We primarily use copper and PEX (cross-linked polyethylene) piping. PEX is flexible, resistant to corrosion, and cost-effective. Copper is durable and time-tested. We'll recommend the best option for your home and budget.

faqs.question: How long does a whole-home repipe take?
faqs.answer: A typical whole-home repipe takes 2–5 days depending on the size of your home and complexity of the plumbing system. We minimize disruption and keep you informed throughout the process.

faqs.question: Will repiping increase my home's value?
faqs.answer: Yes. Repiping with modern materials improves water quality, increases water pressure, and eliminates leak risks — all of which add value to your home and provide peace of mind for potential buyers.
